summary: Dependency-confusion exfiltration probe targeting UIDAI namespace
details: On `npm install`, the package's `preinstall` script (package.json line 7) runs an inline Node one-liner that collects the installer's hostname, OS username, `NODE_ENV` value, and current working directory, URL-encodes them into a DNS subdomain of `d8of5iqplbq2f51eve30ehoxccgnj8fur.oast.site` (an out-of-band interaction server), and issues an HTTP GET so the host identifiers are exfiltrated via DNS and HTTP to the attacker. The package is otherwise hollow — it ships only an empty `index.js` and self-describes as 'UIDAI Reusable UI Components - PoC for dependency confusion', confirming it exists solely to be resolved in place of an internal `uidai_reusable_components` package on UIDAI/Aadhaar build systems and to beacon out from any CI that mistakenly pulls the public version. The harm fires automatically on default install with no user consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
